min_age_pharmacist_vax: 19+ for ACIP-recommended vaccines. No min age for other vaccines per collaborative practice agreement or prescription.
Pharmacist can independently prescribe and administer vaccines for all ages.

Pharmacists can adminsiter influenza vaccines for children age 3 or older. All ACIP recommended vaccines may be administered to children 6 or older. Vaccines recommended for international travel may be administered to individuals 18 and older. Lastly, pharmacists may prescribe and administer for children age 3 or older.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists can adiminster vaccines and prescibe to children age 3 and older
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

basis_of_coverage_mandate: Jan 1, 2025 Federal Baseline (ACIP, USPSTF, HRSA), CDPH Recommendations, AAP, ACOG, AAFP Clinical Guidelines
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in California can independently prescribe and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals aged 3 and older. They may also administer vaccines to all ages under a physician-approved protocol.
Pharmacists in Colorado may administer all vaccines to individuals of any age. This must be done under a collaborative practice agreement or a physician-approved protocol.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

basis_of_coverage_mandate: Clinical Guidelines (AAP, AAFP, ACOG) and ACIP
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Connecticut can independently prescribe and administer CDC Adult Immunization Schedule vaccines for individuals 12+ and additional vaccines listed on the CDC’s website for those 18+. They may also administer vaccines to all ages with a patient-specific prescription.

alternative_bodies: ACIP, AAP, AAFP, ACOG, ACP, IDSA, SMFM

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Delaware may administer vaccines to individuals age 3 and older under a practitioner or physician-approved protocol. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

basis_of_coverage_mandate: Previous ACIP recommendations (Dec 31, 2024); Clinical Guidelines (AAP, AAFP, ACOG)
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in the District of Columbia may independently prescribe and administer all CDC-recommended vaccines for individuals age 3 and older. They may also administer vaccines to all ages with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in Florida may administer vaccines on the CDC Adult Immunization Schedule or for international travel to individuals age 7 and older under a physician-approved protocol. The Board of Pharmacy may authorize additional vaccines, but none have been approved at this time.

Pharmacists in Georgia may administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 13+ and other ACIP-recommended vaccines to those 18+ under a physician-approved protocol. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.
Pharmacists in Hawaii may independently prescribe and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 3 and older.
Pharmacists may prescribe and administer all vaccines to indivudals of all ages.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Illinois may independently administer COVID-19 and influenza vaccines. They may also administer all CDC-recommended vaccines to individuals age 7 and older under a physician standing order.

Pharmacists in Indiana may administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 11 and older under a statewide standing order. They may also administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in Iowa may independently prescribe and administer influenza vaccines for individuals 6 months+ and other CDC Adult Immunization Schedule vaccines (including Tdap and COVID-19) for those 18+. They may also administer the final two doses of the HPV vaccine to individuals age 11 and older.
Pharmacists in Kansas may administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 6+ and all ACIP-recommended vaccines to those 12+ under a vaccination protocol with a licensed physician.

Pharmacists in Kentucky may administer vaccines to individuals age 5 and older under a prescriber-approved protocol. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in Louisiana may independently prescribe and administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 7+ and other ACIP-recommended vaccines to those 17+. They may also administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

basis_of_coverage_mandate: Clinical Guidelines (AAP, AAFP, ACOG) ; Maine Vaccine Board Determination
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Maine may administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals age 3+ and other ACIP-recommended vaccines to those 18+ under a treatment protocol with a pharmacist through a collaborative practice agreement. They may also administer other FDA-approved vaccines to individuals 18+ with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in Maryland may independently order and administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines used in a public health emergency for individuals age 3+. They may also administer other FDA-approved vaccines to individuals 7+ and CDC-recommended travel vaccines to those 18+.

basis_of_coverage_mandate: Clinical Guidelines (AAP, AAFP, ACOG); Commissioner Determination/State Guidance for Childhood Vaccines
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Massachusetts may administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals age 5+ under a practitioner’s order. They may also administer all other vaccines to individuals 18+ under a collaborative practice agreement.

Pharmacists in Michigan may independently order and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 3+. They may also administer other FDA-licensed vaccines under a collaborative practice agreement.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Minnesota may independently prescribe and administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals age 3+. They may also administer all other ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 6 and older.

Pharmacists in Mississippi may administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription. They may also administer vaccines under a collaborative practice agreement with a physician in institutional settings or for individual patients.

Pharmacists in Missouri may independently prescribe and administer FDA-approved vaccines to individuals age 7 and older, excluding vaccines for cholera, monkeypox, Japanese encephalitis, typhoid, rabies, yellow fever, tick-borne encephalitis, anthrax, tuberculosis, dengue, Hib, polio, rotavirus, smallpox, and any vaccine approved after January 1, 2023. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in Montana may independently prescribe and administer influenza vaccines to individuals 12+, pneumococcal/Tdap vaccines to those 18+, and shingles vaccines to all ages per CDC guidance. They may also prescribe and administer ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 7+ under a collaborative practice agreement.
Pharmacists in Nebraska may prescribe and administer vaccines under a practice agreement and written protocol with a licensed healthcare practitioner.
Pharmacists in Nevada may administer vaccines under written guidelines and protocols developed in collaboration with a practitioner.

Pharmacists in New Hampshire may administer COVID-19 and influenza vaccines to individuals of all ages. They may also administer all other ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals 18+ under a standing order or prescription.

In New Jersey, pharmacists may currently order and administer COVID-19 and childhood vaccines to individuals age 3+ under a temporary administrative order. They may also administer any vaccine under an authorized prescriber’s standing order, with expanded authority for COVID-19 and influenza vaccines to individuals 5+ pending rulemaking.

basis_of_coverage_mandate: Department of Health Recommendations (which are based on AAP, AAFP, ACOG, ACP)
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in New Mexico may independently prescribe and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals of all ages in accordance with CDC guidance.

alternative_bodies: ACIP, AAP, AAFP, ACOG, VIP, NMDOH

basis_of_coverage_mandate: NYS Commissioner of Health/Northeast Public Health Collaborative Recommendations
min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in New York may administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 2+ and a specified list of vaccines (including pneumococcal, shingles, hepatitis A/B, HPV, MMR, varicella, COVID-19, meningococcal, and Tdap) to those 18+ under a non-patient-specific order. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in North Carolina may independently prescribe and administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals of all ages (combination vaccines only for those 7+). They may also administer ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals 18+ under a physician protocol or to those 6+ (and other vaccines for 18+) with a patient-specific prescription.
Pharmacists in North Dakota may prescribe and administer all CDC-recommended vaccines to individuals age 3 and older.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Ohio may administer all vaccines to individuals age 5 and older under a physician-approved protocol.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Oklahoma may independently prescribe and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals of all ages.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Oregon may independently prescribe and administer influenza vaccines for individuals age 6 months+ and all other ACIP-recommended vaccines (including those in the CDC’s Pink and Yellow Books) for individuals age 7 and older.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Pennsylvania may administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals age 5+ and all other ACIP-recommended vaccines to those 8+ under a written protocol with a physician.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Rhode Island may administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 9+ and all other ACIP- or AAP-recommended vaccines to those 18+ under a physician-approved protocol.

Pharmacists in South Carolina may independently prescribe and administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 3+ and a specified list of vaccines (including Hib, Hep A/B, HPV, MMR, meningococcal, pneumococcal, Td/Tdap, varicella, zoster, COVID-19, and RSV) to those 18+. They may also administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in South Dakota may independently prescribe and administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 18 and older. They may also administer all other vaccines under a physician-approved protocol.

Pharmacists in Tennessee may independently prescribe and administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals age 3+ and all other vaccines to those 18+. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.

Pharmacists in Texas may administer influenza vaccines to individuals age 7+ and all other vaccines to those 14+ under a physician’s written protocol. They may also administer all vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Utah may administer a specified list of vaccines (including influenza, HepA/B, MMR, HPV, COVID-19, Tdap, pneumococcal, RSV, and others) to individuals of all ages under a signed Vaccine Administration Protocol. They may also administer all other ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Vermont may independently prescribe and administer influenza and COVID-19 vaccines to individuals age 5 and older. They may also administer all other ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 18 and older.

Pharmacists in Virginia may independently prescribe and administer all CDC-recommended vaccines to individuals age 3 and older. They may also administer vaccines to individuals of any age with a patient-specific prescription.
Pharmacists in Washington may administer all vaccines under a Collaborative Drug Therapy Agreement or a patient-specific prescription.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in West Virginia may independently prescribe and administer all CDC-recommended vaccines to individuals age 3 and older.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Wisconsin may independently prescribe and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 6+. They may also administer other vaccines to individuals 6+ under a statewide standing order or physician-approved protocol, and all vaccines to any age with a patient-specific prescription.
exemptions_allowed: Allows religious, personal belief and medical exemptions

min_age_pharmacist_vax: Pharmacists in Wyoming may independently prescribe and administer all ACIP-recommended vaccines to individuals age 7 and older.
